title: PHP Date and Time
content:
### PHP Date and Time

PHP provides powerful functions for date and time manipulation.

### date() Function

```php
<?php
echo date("Y-m-d H:i:s");      // 2026-07-11 22:30:00
echo date("l, F j, Y");        // Saturday, July 11, 2026
echo date("g:i A");            // 10:30 PM
echo date("\T\o\d\a\y \i\s l"); // Today is Saturday
?>
```

### Common Format Characters

| Char | Description | Example |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `d` | Day (01-31) | `11` |
| `D` | Day short | `Sat` |
| `l` | Day full | `Saturday` |
| `m` | Month (01-12) | `07` |
| `M` | Month short | `Jul` |
| `F` | Month full | `July` |
| `Y` | Year (4 digit) | `2026` |
| `y` | Year (2 digit) | `26` |
| `H` | Hour (00-23) | `22` |
| `i` | Minutes (00-59) | `30` |
| `s` | Seconds (00-59) | `00` |
| `A` | AM/PM | `PM` |

### time() and strtotime()

```php
<?php
echo time(); // Unix timestamp: 1777069800

echo strtotime("now");
echo strtotime("+1 day");
echo strtotime("next Monday");
echo strtotime("last day of this month");
echo date("Y-m-d", strtotime("+2 weeks"));
?>
```

### DateTime Class (PHP 5.2+)

```php
<?php
$date = new DateTime();
echo $date->format("Y-m-d H:i:s");

$date->modify("+1 day");
$date->setDate(2026, 12, 25);
$date->setTime(10, 30, 0);

$interval = new DateInterval("P1M"); // 1 month
$date->add($interval);

$now = new DateTime();
$diff = $now->diff($date);
echo $diff->days . " days remaining";
?>
```

> **Key Point:** Use the `DateTime` class for complex date manipulation. It provides better OOP interface and immutability with `DateTimeImmutable`.
codeExample:
format("Y-m-d H:i:s") . "
"; // Date comparison $deadline = new DateTime("2026-08-01"); $today = new DateTime("now"); if ($today < $deadline) { $remaining = $today->diff($deadline); echo "Deadline in: {$remaining->days} days, {$remaining->h} hours
"; } // strtotime examples echo "Next week: " . date("Y-m-d", strtotime("+1 week")) . "
"; echo "Next month: " . date("Y-m-d", strtotime("+1 month")) . "
"; echo "Last Monday: " . date("Y-m-d", strtotime("last Monday")) . "
"; // Timezone $tz = new DateTimeZone("America/New_York"); $nyTime = new DateTime("now", $tz); echo "NY time: " . $nyTime->format("Y-m-d H:i:s") . "
"; // Unix timestamp echo "Timestamp: " . time() . "
"; echo "From timestamp: " . date("Y-m-d", 1777069800) . "
"; ?>

quiz.question: Which function creates a DateTime from a relative string like '+1 day'?
